html_content:
Ola Electric — Strategy Knowledge Graph

> Extraction Date: 2026-05-15
> Sources: DRHP, Screener Financials, Competition Analysis, Industry Research, Post-IPO Analysis, VAHAN Registration Data
> Latest Market Cap: ₹15,923 Cr | Stock Price: ₹36.1 | Total Notes: 107

---

Overview

Companies/Ola Electric is a pure EV player in India building vertically integrated technology and manufacturing capabilities. Founded by Companies/Bhavish Aggarwal, it operates India's largest company-owned EV experience centre network (935 centres) and is building an integrated EV hub in Tamil Nadu.

---

Critical Path to Success

1. Achieve positive unit economics through Strategy/Backward Integration and Strategy/Operating Leverage
2. Execute Gigafactory — Ola Gigafactory must hit 1→5→10→20 GWh milestones
3. Defend market share against Companies/TVS Motor, Companies/Bajaj Auto, Companies/Hero MotoCorp, Companies/Ather Energy
4. Manage cash burn — cumulative losses exceed ₹47,000M through FY25
5. Secure continued policy support — Market/EV Policy subsidies remain critical

---

Key Metrics at a Glance

| Metric | FY22 | FY23 | FY24 | FY25 |
| ----------------------- | ----- | ------ | ------ | ------ |
| Revenue from Ops (₹ Cr) | 373 | 2,631 | 5,010 | 4,514 |
| Net Loss (₹ Cr) | -784 | -1,472 | -1,584 | -2,276 |
| Adj Gross Margin % | -5.4% | 7.6% | — | — |
| Borrowings (₹ Cr) | 2,608 | 3,505 | 5,684 | 3,556 |

---

Navigation (97 files across 7 folders + root)

Companies (15 files)

  • Companies/Ola Electric — Full company profile, pure EV manufacturer, Rs 15,923 Cr mcap
  • Companies/Bhavish Aggarwal — Founder & Promoter, 36.94% stake, DIN 03287473
  • Companies/Ather Energy — Premium pure EV competitor, 16% share, 75% YoY growth
  • Companies/TVS Motor — iQube, E2W market leader at 23% share, 371K units FY26
  • Companies/Bajaj Auto — Chetak, Rs 400/visit service, Rs 38,552 Cr reserves
  • Companies/Hero MotoCorp — Vida V1, 6,000+ dealers, 5-year warranty, sleeping giant
  • Companies/SoftBank Vision Fund — 21.98% stakeholder at Rs 50.94 cost basis, 29% underwater
  • Companies/Tiger Global — 6.03% stakeholder at Rs 11.70 cost basis, 208% gain
  • Companies/Competitor_Firepower — Side-by-side financial firepower comparison
  • Companies/Shareholding_Pattern_Analysis — FII 5.41%→3.99%, retail 54%, SoftBank overhang
  • Companies/TVS iQube — Primary competitor product: 95% parts, 1-2 day turnaround
  • Companies/Bajaj Chetak — Only profitable EV scooter in India, metal body, TPM-certified
  • Companies/Ather Grid — 2,000+ charging points, most extensive public E2W network
  • Companies/Ola Fleet Technologies — Related party entity, Rs 722.6 Cr FY23 fee, in-housed Jul 2023
  • Companies/Ola Financial Services — Captive vehicle financing, 12 lending partners

Risks (16 files)

  • Risks/Financial Losses — Cumulative losses >Rs 6,315 Cr FY21-FY25, no net profit path
  • Risks/Subsidy Dependency — FAME→EMPS→PM E-DRIVE→cliff Jul 2026, 77% subsidy reduction
  • Risks/Gigafactory Execution — 1→20 GWh with 2x penalty clauses, 16% utilization
  • Risks/Product Quality — 189 consumer cases pre-IPO, 500+ by May 2026
  • Risks/Supply Chain — 35% BOM from imported cells, LFP pivot addresses cobalt risk
  • Risks/Competition — Legacy OEMs (TVS 23%, Bajaj 21%) captured 65.23% combined share
  • Risks/Customer Experience Gap — Service & quality friction, 7-15 day turnaround
  • Risks/Revenue Decline FY25 — 9.9% revenue drop FY25, 52% unit decline FY26
  • Risks/Service_Center_Crisis — D2C overwhelmed at 100K+ volume, CCPA show-cause Oct 2024
  • Risks/Customer_Sentiment_Analysis — NPS negative, Google Play 3.2, social media backlash
  • Risks/CCPA_Regulatory_Intervention — 10,000+ complaints, show-cause notice, 500+ consumer cases
  • Risks/Consumer Court Crisis — 500+ court cases, bailable warrants, "pattern of neglect"
  • Risks/Acquisition Target Risk — Rs 15,923 Cr mcap vs Gigafactory asset value, retailification
  • Risks/D2C Model Fixed Cost Trap — SG&A 35.4% of revenue, 42% OpEx cut for survival
  • Risks/Ola Product Mix ASP Trap — S1 X 75% of mix destroyed ASP from Rs 135K to Rs 89K
  • Risks/Service_Center_Crisis — (stale duplicate removed — see Service_Center_Crisis)

Financial (9 files)

  • Financial/Revenue Trajectory — Growth then 71% peak-to-trough decline, FY26 stabilization
  • Financial/Quarterly Losses — Cumulative Rs 6,315 Cr losses FY21-FY25, 10 quarters tracked
  • Financial/Balance Sheet Analysis — Rising stress, Rs 3,516 Cr cash, 1.5-year runway
  • Financial/Cash Flow Analysis — Persistent negative CFO, Rs 5,639 Cr investing outflows
  • Financial/IPO Proceeds — Rs 5,500 Cr fresh issue, OFS 95.19M shares, allocation details
  • Financial/Unit Economics — BOM, battery as 35% of cost, operating leverage post-Gigafactory
  • Financial/Post-IPO_Updates — Operational reality check Aug 2024-May 2026
  • Financial/Post-IPO_Quarterly_Calls — Management commentary across 8 quarters
  • Financial/Margin_Paradox — Revenue down 34% but gross margin 18%→34.3%→36%, breakeven 15K units

Market (21 files)

  • Market/E2W Market India — $35B→$45B at 11% CAGR, 6.9x market growth Apr 2022-Mar 2026
  • Market/EV Policy — FAME, PLI, GST, state incentives, PM E-DRIVE terminal date Jul 2026
  • Market/TCO Advantage — 45% lower than ICE, narrowing post-subsidy
  • Market/Policy_Regulatory_Timeline — Full chronology of policy changes 2019-2026
  • Market/Battery_Supply_Chain — Global 4,200 GWh capacity, 900 GWh excess, cobalt spike
  • Market/Export_Markets_Detail — Africa 380K, SE Asia 400K, LATAM 600K TAM
  • Market/Subsidy_Cliff_Jul2026 — PM E-DRIVE expiry Jul 31 2026, Karnataka tax reversal
  • Market/VAHAN_Market_Share_Story — Ola 55.81%→8.6%→15.95%, legacy 65.23%, 48-month timeline
  • Market/PM E-DRIVE Subsidy — Current Rs 5,000/E2W scheme, DVA requirement, fund-limited
  • Market/FAME_II_Scheme — Historical Rs 40,000/E2W scheme (2019-2024), 87.5% reduction context
  • Market/Brand Trust Deficit — Trust meta-concept linking service crisis→sales collapse→recovery
  • Market/Brand Perception — Quality perception, premium vs budget image, competitor comparison
  • Market/Google Play Store Rating — 4.0→3.2 rating decline, quantitative sentiment proxy
  • Market/Net_Promoter_Score — NPS negative vs Bajaj 40-50, structural word-of-mouth headwind
  • Market/Social_Media_Sentiment — Twitter/Reddit/YouTube amplification of service complaints
  • Market/EV_Warranty_Competition — Hero 5+5yr best, Ola 3+3yr weakest, 5.3x provisions growth
  • Market/Battery_Raw_Materials — Cobalt +133% to $56K/MT, lithium V-shaped recovery
  • Market/State_EV_Policies — TN/Karnataka/Gujarat/Delhi/Maharashtra policy patchwork
  • Market/VAHAN Registration Data — Government registration data methodology and interpretation
  • Market/Legacy OEM Structural Advantage — Dealer networks, TPM manufacturing, cross-subsidy firepower
  • Market/Chinese OEM Export Competition — 900 GWh oversupply, export market dominance threat

Opportunities (11 files)

  • Opportunities/Mass Market E2W — S1 X series at Rs 69,999, 75% of sales mix
  • Opportunities/Cell Manufacturing — 20 GWh PLI, B2B cell supply, energy storage pivot
  • Opportunities/Motorcycle Market — 65% of 2W market, 4 models announced
  • Opportunities/Export Markets — Africa, LATAM, SE Asia, $2-3B TAM by 2028
  • Opportunities/PLI Dual Beneficiary — Only EV maker with both Auto PLI and Cell PLI
  • Opportunities/D2C Network Moat — India's largest EV-owned network, 935 centres
  • Opportunities/Platform Evolution — Gen 3 shipping Mar 2025, Gen 4 next evolution
  • Opportunities/Ola Shakti — Residential/commercial storage, B2B cell supply pivot
  • Opportunities/S1 X — The margin paradox product: lower ASP, higher GP/unit
  • Opportunities/Roadster — First motorcycle entering production FY26, 2% Q4 FY26 mix
  • Opportunities/Motorcycle Product Line — Diamondhead, Adventure, Roadster, Cruiser lineup

Strategy (7 files)

  • Strategy/Backward Integration — Cell manufacturing + supplier colocation, battery BOM leverage
  • Strategy/D2C Omnichannel — 935 experience centres, 414 service centres, D2C economics
  • Strategy/EV Hub Tamil Nadu — 417 acres integrated manufacturing, Futurefactory 1M/yr capacity
  • Strategy/Operating Leverage — OpEx slashed 42%, breakeven lowered to 15K units/month
  • Strategy/Product_Mix_Cannibalization — S1 X 75% of mix, ASP crashed 34% but GP/unit rose 26%
  • Strategy/B2B_Battery_Pivot — Ola Shakti, OEM cell supply, 16% utilization forcing pivot
  • Strategy/Service Recovery Initiatives — Project Vistaar, Hyperservice, HyperDelivery, Project Lakshya

Technology (11 files)

  • Technology/Gen 2 Platform — 68.85% component commonality across S1 series
  • Technology/4680 Cell Tech — NMC 4680 form factor (historical), superseded by LFP 46100
  • Technology/MoveOS — In-house connected vehicle OS, v4 with OTA updates
  • Technology/R&D Investment — Rs 5,077M in FY23, centres in India/UK/US, BIC
  • Technology/Gigafactory_Updates — 6 GWh capacity, LFP 46100 at $80/kWh, 30% BOM reduction
  • Technology/Gen_3_Platform — Shipping Mar 2025, S1 at ~Rs 89,000, 3.97 kWh battery
  • Technology/LFP_46100_Cell — NMC→LFP pivot, cobalt-free, thermal stability 45C+, 3,000+ cycles
  • Technology/BaaS — Battery-as-a-Service model for Africa exports, 18% GST penalty
  • Technology/Battery Innovation Centre — R&D hub, cell technology development, TN EV Hub
  • Technology/Cobalt Supply Risk and LFP Pivot — Cobalt supply risk analysis + LFP vs NMC comparison
  • Technology/Global Cell Price Parity — Ola at $80/kWh import parity, make-vs-buy analysis

Solutions (10 files) — 🆕 The Big Brand Theory Competition

  • Solutions/The Ola Turnaround Strategy — Central hub: 5-pillar, 30-month turnaround architecture
  • Solutions/Comprehensive Market Analysis — Evidence synthesis: market structure, competition, challenges, opportunity spaces
  • Solutions/GTM Strategy Project Horizon — 4-phase GTM: Halt & Harden → Reliable Relaunch → Motorcycle Entry → Export Offensive
  • Solutions/Marketing Campaign Radical Transparency — Engineer-led content, live service dashboard, factory live-stream, recall promise
  • Solutions/Decentralized Service Network — Hub & Spoke model: 300 Ola hubs + 2,000 certified local garages
  • Solutions/Zero-Tolerance QA — 100% battery thermal testing, 500km PDI, 48-hour recall promise
  • Solutions/Post-Subsidy Pricing Architecture — Surviving the Jul 2026 subsidy cliff: cost hardening + state arbitrage + mix optimization
  • Solutions/Battery-First Strategic Pivot — Gigafactory as primary growth engine: B2B cells + Ola Shakti storage
  • Solutions/Captive Financing and Affordability — Battery leasing, pay-per-km, Ola Care subscription
  • Solutions/Investor Confidence Restoration — Stock recovery from Rs 29 to Rs 67+, strategic capital entry

Root Notes (7 files)

  • Cash burn — Cash runway analysis, burn rate, funding requirement timeline
  • Chetak — Bajaj Chetak competitive analysis, Rs 400/visit service moat
  • Graphite — Graphite supply chain and battery raw material context
  • interest — Interest cost trajectory, debt structure, borrowing rates 8.75-10.90%
  • Social media — Social media sentiment channels and brand damage quantification
  • Companies/SoftBank Vision Fund — SoftBank investment analysis, lockup expiry, 21.98% stake overhang
  • Units sold — Unit sales trajectory, volume collapse, recovery indicators

Data Sources

DATA: Source: E2W_MONTHLY_REGISTRATIONS.csv
Ola Electric's market share collapsed from a peak of 55.81% in Apr-22 to 9.71% by Sep-25, a loss of 46.1 percentage points over 41 months. The total market expanded 6.9x from 21,500 units (Apr-22) to 148,713 units (Mar-26), but Ola failed to capture any of that growth — its peak volume was 53,320 units in Mar-24, which collapsed to 7,567 by Nov-25 (86% decline).
Causal chain: Peak market share→service complaints overwhelm→regulatory action→consumer trust collapse→continuous decline→restructuring
Cells: Row 2 Ola_Market_Share_Pct=55.81; Row 43 Ola_Market_Share_Pct=9.71; Row 25 Ola=53320; Row 45 Ola=7567; Row 49 Total_Market=148713
DATA: Source: E2W_MONTHLY_REGISTRATIONS.csv
Legacy players (TVS+Bajaj) surpassed Ola's combined market share for the first time in Feb-24 — Legacy 42.51% vs Ola 41.92%. By Mar-26, legacy share reached 65.23% while Ola was at 15.95%, a complete reversal of market leadership in 24 months.
Causal chain: Service complaints favor legacy→consumers switch→legacy captures share→Ola declines→irreversible reversal
Cells: Row 24 Legacy_Market_Share_Pct=42.51, Ola_Market_Share_Pct=41.92; Row 49 Legacy_Market_Share_Pct=65.23, Ola_Market_Share_Pct=15.95
DATA: Source: ola/BALANCE_SHEET.csv, ola/CASH_FLOW_AND_DERIVED.csv
Ola Electric's cash runway is critically short: Rs 3,516 Cr cash at FY25-end, burning Rs 2,391 Cr annually from operations. At this rate, Ola has approximately 1.5 years of runway before needing emergency funding. Cumulative losses over 5 years total Rs 6,315 Cr.
Causal chain: Continuous losses→accumulated deficit→funding requirement→dilution or debt→further stock pressure→acquisition opportunity at depressed valuation
Cells: Row 14, Col F (Mar-25) = 3516; CASH_FLOW Row 2, Col F (Mar-25) = -2391; Row 15 Net profit FY21-FY25
DATA: Source: E2W_MONTHLY_REGISTRATIONS.csv
Ola's volume collapse from 53,320 units (Mar-24, its all-time peak) to 7,567 units (Nov-25, its nadir) represents an 86% volume destruction in 20 months — the most severe volume decline by any major OEM in Indian E2W history. The entire EV segment grew ~30% in FY26 while Ola declined 52% YoY.
Causal chain: Peak demand→service failure→regulatory crisis→volume collapse→survival restructuring→fragile stabilization at 36% of peak volume
Cells: Row 25 Ola=53320; Row 45 Ola=7567; market/COMPETITOR_EV_METRICS Row 3: Ola_FY25=340000, Ola_FY26=164000
